intro: Sarothra graminea, also known as the Borneo stinkwood, is a plant species native to the tropical rainforests of Borneo. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Sarothra graminea include lignans, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Sarothra graminea include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, from minor skin irritations to more serious infections.
